title: banksia rufa subsp. flavescens
intro: Banksia rufa subsp. flavescens is a subspecies of the Banksia rufa tree, native to Western Australia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and narrow leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to have a range of health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections. The bark, leaves, and flowers of the plant are used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects when consumed. In addition to its medicinal properties, the plant is also valued for its role in stabilizing soil and providing habitat for native wildlife.
